item.title: SpecEyes: Accelerating Agentic Multimodal LLMs via Speculative Perception and Planning
item.description: Agentic multimodal large language models (MLLMs) (e.g., OpenAI o3 and Gemini Agentic Vision) achieve remarkable reasoning capabilities through iterative visual tool invocation. However, the cascaded perception, reasoning, and tool-callin...

item.title: Ego2Web: A Web Agent Benchmark Grounded in Egocentric Videos
item.description: Multimodal AI agents are increasingly automating complex real-world workflows that involve online web execution. However, current web-agent benchmarks suffer from a critical limitation: they focus entirely on web-based interaction and pe...

item.title: From Static Templates to Dynamic Runtime Graphs: A Survey of Workflow Optimization for LLM Agents
item.description: Large language model (LLM)-based systems are becoming increasingly popular for solving tasks by constructing executable workflows that interleave LLM calls, information retrieval, tool use, code execution, memory updates, and verificatio...

item.title: MinerU-Diffusion: Rethinking Document OCR as Inverse Rendering via Diffusion Decoding
item.description: Optical character recognition (OCR) has evolved from line-level transcription to structured document parsing, requiring models to recover long-form sequences containing layout, tables, and formulas. Despite recent advances in vision-lang...

item.title: ABot-PhysWorld: Interactive World Foundation Model for Robotic Manipulation with Physics Alignment
item.description: Video-based world models offer a powerful paradigm for embodied simulation and planning, yet state-of-the-art models often generate physically implausible manipulations - such as object penetration and anti-gravity motion - due to traini...

item.title: PEARL: Personalized Streaming Video Understanding Model
item.description: Human cognition of new concepts is inherently a streaming process: we continuously recognize new objects or identities and update our memories over time. However, current multimodal personalization methods are largely limited to static i...

item.title: AgentSLR: Automating Systematic Literature Reviews in Epidemiology with Agentic AI
item.description: Systematic literature reviews are essential for synthesizing scientific evidence but are costly, difficult to scale and time-intensive, creating bottlenecks for evidence-based policy. We study whether large language models can automate t...

item.title: VISion On Request: Enhanced VLLM efficiency with sparse, dynamically selected, vision-language interactions
item.description: Existing approaches for improving the efficiency of Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs) are largely based on the concept of visual token reduction. This approach, however, creates an information bottleneck that impairs performance, espe...

item.title: Uncertainty-guided Compositional Alignment with Part-to-Whole Semantic Representativeness in Hyperbolic Vision-Language Models
item.description: While Vision-Language Models (VLMs) have achieved remarkable performance, their Euclidean embeddings remain limited in capturing hierarchical relationships such as part-to-whole or parent-child structures, and often face challenges in mu...

item.title: Session Risk Memory (SRM): Temporal Authorization for Deterministic Pre-Execution Safety Gates
item.description: Deterministic pre-execution safety gates evaluate whether individual agent actions are compatible with their assigned roles. While effective at per-action authorization, these systems are structurally blind to distributed attacks that de...

item.title: RealMaster: Lifting Rendered Scenes into Photorealistic Video
item.description: State-of-the-art video generation models produce remarkable photorealism, but they lack the precise control required to align generated content with specific scene requirements. Furthermore, without an underlying explicit geometry, these...

item.title: Rethinking Token-Level Policy Optimization for Multimodal Chain-of-Thought
item.description: Multimodal Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning requires large vision-language models to construct reasoning trajectories that interleave perceptual grounding with multi-step inference. However, existing Reinforcement Learning with Verifiabl...

item.title: Attend Before Attention: Efficient and Scalable Video Understanding via Autoregressive Gazing
item.description: Multi-modal large language models (MLLMs) have advanced general-purpose video understanding but struggle with long, high-resolution videos -- they process every pixel equally in their vision transformers (ViTs) or LLMs despite significan...

item.title: Fair splits flip the leaderboard: CHANRG reveals limited generalization in RNA secondary-structure prediction
item.description: Accurate prediction of RNA secondary structure underpins transcriptome annotation, mechanistic analysis of non-coding RNAs, and RNA therapeutic design. Recent gains from deep learning and RNA foundation models are difficult to interpret...

item.title: Reasoning or Rhetoric? An Empirical Analysis of Moral Reasoning Explanations in Large Language Models
item.description: Do large language models reason morally, or do they merely sound like they do? We investigate whether LLM responses to moral dilemmas exhibit genuine developmental progression through Kohlberg's stages of moral development, or whether al...

item.title: Can AI Agents Answer Your Data Questions? A Benchmark for Data Agents
item.description: Users across enterprises increasingly rely on AI agents to query their data through natural language. However, building reliable data agents remains difficult because real-world data is often fragmented across multiple heterogeneous data...

item.title: WildWorld: A Large-Scale Dataset for Dynamic World Modeling with Actions and Explicit State toward Generative ARPG
item.description: Dynamical systems theory and reinforcement learning view world evolution as latent-state dynamics driven by actions, with visual observations providing partial information about the state. Recent video world models attempt to learn this...

item.title: DA-Flow: Degradation-Aware Optical Flow Estimation with Diffusion Models
item.description: Optical flow models trained on high-quality data often degrade severely when confronted with real-world corruptions such as blur, noise, and compression artifacts. To overcome this limitation, we formulate Degradation-Aware Optical Flow,...

item.title: SIMART: Decomposing Monolithic Meshes into Sim-ready Articulated Assets via MLLM
item.description: High-quality articulated 3D assets are indispensable for embodied AI and physical simulation, yet 3D generation still focuses on static meshes, leaving a gap in "sim-ready" interactive objects. Most recent articulated object creation met...

item.title: VP-VLA: Visual Prompting as an Interface for Vision-Language-Action Models
item.description: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models typically map visual observations and linguistic instructions directly to robotic control signals. This "black-box" mapping forces a single forward pass to simultaneously handle instruction interpretat...

item.title: CanViT: Toward Active-Vision Foundation Models
item.description: Active computer vision promises efficient, biologically plausible perception through sequential, localized glimpses, but lacks scalable general-purpose architectures and pretraining pipelines. As a result, Active-Vision Foundation Models...

item.title: MultiBind: A Benchmark for Attribute Misbinding in Multi-Subject Generation
item.description: Subject-driven image generation is increasingly expected to support fine-grained control over multiple entities within a single image. In multi-reference workflows, users may provide several subject images, a background reference, and lo...

item.title: TrajLoom: Dense Future Trajectory Generation from Video
item.description: Predicting future motion is crucial in video understanding and controllable video generation. Dense point trajectories are a compact, expressive motion representation, but modeling their future evolution from observed video remains chall...

item.title: Sparse but Critical: A Token-Level Analysis of Distributional Shifts in RLVR Fine-Tuning of LLMs
item.description: Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) has significantly improved reasoning in large language models (LLMs), yet the token-level mechanisms underlying these improvements remain unclear. We present a systematic empirical st...

item.title: Abstraction as a Memory-Efficient Inductive Bias for Continual Learning
item.description: The real world is non-stationary and infinitely complex, requiring intelligent agents to learn continually without the prohibitive cost of retraining from scratch. While online continual learning offers a framework for this setting, lear...

item.title: Regulating AI Agents
item.description: AI agents -- systems that can independently take actions to pursue complex goals with only limited human oversight -- have entered the mainstream. These systems are now being widely used to produce software, conduct business activities,...

item.title: STEM Agent: A Self-Adapting, Tool-Enabled, Extensible Architecture for Multi-Protocol AI Agent Systems
item.description: Current AI agent frameworks commit early to a single interaction protocol, a fixed tool integration strategy, and static user models, limiting their deployment across diverse interaction paradigms. To address these constraints, we introd...

item.title: SHAMISA: SHAped Modeling of Implicit Structural Associations for Self-supervised No-Reference Image Quality Assessment
item.description: No-Reference Image Quality Assessment (NR-IQA) aims to estimate perceptual quality without access to a reference image of pristine quality. Learning an NR-IQA model faces a fundamental bottleneck: its need for a large number of costly hu...

item.title: UniGRPO: Unified Policy Optimization for Reasoning-Driven Visual Generation
item.description: Unified models capable of interleaved generation have emerged as a promising paradigm, with the community increasingly converging on autoregressive modeling for text and flow matching for image generation. To advance this direction, we p...

item.title: 2Xplat: Two Experts Are Better Than One Generalist
item.description: Pose-free feed-forward 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) has opened a new frontier for rapid 3D modeling, enabling high-quality Gaussian representations to be generated from uncalibrated multi-view images in a single forward pass. The dominan...

item.title: ThinkJEPA: Empowering Latent World Models with Large Vision-Language Reasoning Model
item.description: Recent progress in latent world models (e.g., V-JEPA2) has shown promising capability in forecasting future world states from video observations. Nevertheless, dense prediction from a short observation window limits temporal context and...

item.title: VTAM: Video-Tactile-Action Models for Complex Physical Interaction Beyond VLAs
item.description: Video-Action Models (VAMs) have emerged as a promising framework for embodied intelligence, learning implicit world dynamics from raw video streams to produce temporally consistent action predictions. Although such models demonstrate str...

item.title: One View Is Enough! Monocular Training for In-the-Wild Novel View Generation
item.description: Monocular novel-view synthesis has long required multi-view image pairs for supervision, limiting training data scale and diversity. We argue it is not necessary: one view is enough. We present OVIE, trained entirely on unpaired internet...

item.title: Reconstruction-Guided Slot Curriculum: Addressing Object Over-Fragmentation in Video Object-Centric Learning
item.description: Video Object-Centric Learning seeks to decompose raw videos into a small set of object slots, but existing slot-attention models often suffer from severe over-fragmentation. This is because the model is implicitly encouraged to occupy al...
